verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type 1 transmembrane protein resident in the ER membrane that, upon ER Ca2+ store depletion, translocates and clusters at ER-PM junctions where its cytoplasmic SOAR/CAD domain directly gates Orai1 channels on the plasma membrane. Importantly, STIM1 itself has a short extracellular N-terminal EF-hand/SAM domain that faces the ER lumen and, at ER-PM junctions, is accessible from the extracellular face. Surface biotinylation and antibody-staining studies on intact cells confirm a plasma-membrane-resident pool of STIM1. Its TM domain, extracellular N-terminus, and documented PM surface pool qualify it as a classical single-pass surface protein.
